insight: URLs containing `§` (section sign) — e.g. Dropbox links where `§` decodes to `§` — crash MiniMessage with "Legacy formatting codes have been detected". The root cause is `CompChatColor.convertLegacyToMini()` preserves URLs as-is (including `§`) via URL_PATTERN, and the `§` then reaches `MiniMessage.deserialize()` which rejects it. Fix: strip any remaining `§` from the output of `convertLegacyToMini()` after all valid legacy codes have been converted to MiniMessage tags.
